data.body:
## Description

**What**: A time-boxed spike to validate that the ACLI /latest/ binary download, acli --version extraction, versioned tarball URL reconstruction, and SHA256 computation flow works reliably.
**Why**: The versioned tarball URL pattern is community-reverse-engineered, not officially documented by Atlassian. The acli --version output format is unverified. This spike de-risks SC1 before implementation begins.
**Scope**:
- IN: Download ACLI from /latest/ endpoint, run acli --version, parse version string, construct versioned tarball URL, download tarball, compute SHA256, verify round-trip
- OUT: Integration with project-setup wizard, setting GitHub variables

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, a working bash proof-of-concept script exists that downloads from acli.atlassian.com/latest/, extracts the version string via acli --version, reconstructs the versioned tarball URL, downloads the tarball, and computes its SHA256 — exiting 0 on success
  Satisfies: "the installer auto-resolves ACLI version and SHA256 by downloading the latest ACLI binary from acli.atlassian.com"
- When this story is complete, the version string format emitted by acli --version is documented (whether it includes -stable suffix, what parsing is needed)
  Satisfies: "the installer auto-resolves ACLI version and SHA256"
- When this story is complete, the script has been tested across at least two consecutive ACLI versions to confirm URL pattern stability
  Satisfies: "the installer auto-resolves ACLI version and SHA256"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] ACLI /latest/ endpoint serves a binary, not a tarball — the round-trip requires downloading the binary first, extracting version, then constructing a separate tarball URL
- [Maintainability] Versioned tarball URL pattern is undocumented — if Atlassian changes distribution format, this breaks silently
